finding: KenGen benefits from preferential dispatch arrangements and government-backed financing that are not available to independent power producers (IPPs). This creates an uneven playing field in the generation market.
recommendation: Establish transparent dispatch merit order based on cost; ensure IPPs have equal access to grid connection on same terms as KenGen

finding: KPLC holds a statutory monopoly on electricity distribution. No competitive neutrality concerns as the sector is a regulated natural monopoly. Tariff regulation by ERC provides consumer protection.
recommendation: Maintain ERC oversight; consider introducing competition in large commercial customer segment above 1 MW

finding: Kenya Airways receives government subsidies and guarantees not available to private airlines (Jambojet, Fly540). Government bailout of KES 20 billion distorts competition on domestic routes.
recommendation: Ring-fence government support to international routes only; ensure domestic operations compete on commercial terms; publish subsidy amounts transparently

finding: KPA operates as a landlord port with private terminal operators. Competitive neutrality is generally maintained. However, KPA's own stevedoring operations compete with private operators on non-transparent terms.
recommendation: Separate KPA's regulatory and commercial functions; publish port tariffs and service standards for all operators

finding: KPC holds a monopoly on petroleum pipeline transport. Pipeline tariffs are not independently regulated, creating potential for cross-subsidisation between pipeline and storage services.
recommendation: Establish independent pipeline tariff regulation; separate pipeline and storage accounts; allow third-party access to pipeline on published terms
